SEARCH

Package de.uka.ilkd.key.java

package de.uka.ilkd.key.java
This package contains classes that cover the Java programming language. The classes in the subpackages are mainly taken from the Recoder framework and made immutable. They are transformed into this data structure from a Recoder structure by
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.Recoder2KeY
or
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.SchemaRecoder2KeY
. However, in some details both data structures might differ more. The following explanations are adapted from the documentation of the Recoder framework.
Source and Program Elements
A
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.SourceElement
is a syntactical entity and not necessary a
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.ModelElement
, such as a
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.Comment
.

A

invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.ProgramElement
is a
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.SourceElement
and a
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.ModelElement
. It is aware of its parent in the syntax tree, while a pure
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.SourceElement
is not considered as a member of the AST even though it is represented in the sources.

invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.ProgramElement
s are further classified into
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.TerminalProgramElement
s and
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.NonTerminalProgramElement
s. While
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.TerminalProgramElement
is just a tag class,
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.NonTerminalProgramElement
s know their AST children (while it is possible that they do not have any). A complete source file occurs as a
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.CompilationUnit
.

invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.JavaSourceElement
and
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.JavaProgramElement
are abstract classes defining standard implementations that already know their KeYJavaASTFactory.

Expressions and Statements
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.Expression
and
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.Statement
are self-explanatory. A
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.LoopInitializer
is a special
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.Statement
valid as initializer of
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.statement.For
loops.
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.LoopInitializer
is subtyped by
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.expression.ExpressionStatement
and
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.declaration.LocalVariableDeclaration
).

Concrete classes and further abstractions are bundled in the

invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.expression
and
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.statement
packages.

Syntax Tree Parents
There are a couple of abstractions dealing with properties of being a parent node.

These are

invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.declaration.TypeDeclarationContainer
,
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.ExpressionContainer
,
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.StatementContainer
,
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.ParameterContainer
,
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.NamedProgramElement
and
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.reference.TypeReferenceContainer
. A An
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.ExpressionContainer
contains
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.Expression
s, a
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.StatementContainer
contains
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.Statement
s, a
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.ParameterContainer
(either a
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.declaration.MethodDeclaration
or a
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.statement.Catch
statement) contains
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.declaration.ParameterDeclaration
s. A
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.NamedProgramElement
is a subtype of
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.NamedModelElement
. A
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.reference.TypeReferenceContainer
contains one or several names, but these are names of types that are referred to explicitely by a
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.reference.TypeReference
.

References
A
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.Reference
is an explicite use of an entity. Most of these
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.Reference
s are
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.reference.NameReference
s and as such
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.NamedProgramElement
s, e.g. the
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.reference.TypeReference
. Subtypes of
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.Reference
s are bundled in the
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.reference
package.

Modifiers and Declarations
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.declaration.Modifier
s are (exclusively) used in the context of
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.Declaration
s.
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.declaration.Modifier
s occur explicitly, since they occur as syntactical tokens that might be indented and commented.
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.Declaration
s are either declarations of types or other entities such as
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.declaration.MemberDeclaration
or
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.declaration.VariableDeclaration
. Concrete
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.declaration.Modifier
s and
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.Declaration
s are bundled in the
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.declaration.modifier
and
invalid reference 
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.declaration
packages.

  • Class
    Description
    ConvertException
    This exception class is mainly thrown by Recoder2KeY and its companions.
    JavaInfo
    an instance serves as representation of a Java model underlying a DL formula.
    JavaService
    Facade for the JavaParser.
    JavaTools
    Miscellaneous static methods related to Java blocks or statements in KeY.
    KeYJavaASTFactory
    The KeYASTFactory helps building KeY Java AST structures.
    KeYJPMapping
     
    KeYProgModelInfo
     
    NameAbstractionTable
    This class is used for the equals modulo renaming method in SourceElement.
    Position
    The position of a source element, given by its line and column number.
    ProgramPrefixUtil
     
    ProgramPrefixUtil.ProgramPrefixInfo
     
    ServiceCaches
    Instances of this class provides all caches used by an individual Proof or more precise by its Services.
    Services
    This is a collection of common services to the KeY prover.
    Services.ITermProgramVariableCollectorFactory
